item.title: Magna Carda — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
Austin duo, Magna Carda joins the show to talk inninoutcheamind — the album that brought them back to their jazz roots. Megz Kelli and Dougie Do bring us into their creative process and the vulnerability that shaped this album.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Magna Carda.

item.title: Nighttime — The Indie Show
item.description:
Eva Louise Goodman stops by to talk about the new Nighttime record "Looking Glass" plus music writer Sydney Salk on the state of New York DIY.

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring Nighttime.

item.title: Sharada Shashidhar — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Los Angeles based vocalist, composer and producer Sharada Shashidhar on her new album, A Foot on the Ground

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Sharada Shashidhar.

item.title: Phantom — The Metal Show
item.description:
JC Necrohex, mastermind of Mexican speed metal maniacs Phantom, joins the show to talk about the band's rapid rise.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Phantom.

item.title: Ikonika — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Ikonika joins Emily to talk about their latest release on Club Djembe, why last year's SAD is kind of a debut album and how using their vocals like samples has become an essential part of their music process.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Ikonika and Neil Bopperson.

item.title: The Best Video Game Music of 2026 (So Far) — The Game Show
item.description: Zoe revisits the most crucial soundtracks of the past six months.

item.title: Lifeguard Takeover — The Indie Show
item.description:
Isaac Lowenstein of Lifeguard is your host for the Chicago trio's takeover of The Indie Show, playing tracks from current and old band favorites.

Hosted by Lifeguard.

item.title: Guest Host: Lovie — Bandcamp Selects
item.description: Lovie returns to guest host Bandcamp Selects with another killer offering of jazz and soul.

item.title: Sleepbomb — The Metal Show
item.description:
Tim Gotch and Charlie Hernandez from the San Francisco "cinematic doom" band Sleepbomb join the show to talk about their heavy movie scores.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Sleepbomb.

item.title: Machinedrum — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Machinedrum joins Emily to talk about his mini LP 'Blooms', his first independent release in 13 years.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Machinedrum and MzRizk.

item.title: Moyses Dos Santos — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
London-based Brazilian bassist Moyses Dos Santos on his long-awaited debut album, Maria

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Moyses Dos Santos.

item.title: Well Done — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
Nappy Nina & Swarvy join the show to discuss "Sow & So" — proof that this duo is simply unmatched. Thirteen tracks, unparalleled lyricism, a constellation of legends, noteworthy production, and a sonic world that sounds true to Nina's home.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Nappy Nina & Swarvy.

item.title: feeble little horse — The Indie Show
item.description:
feeble little horse talk about surprise new release "bitknot" plus new tracks from Artificial Go, Eddy Current Suppression Ring, Caroline Rose, and more.

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring feeble little horse.

item.title: Martyn — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Martyn stops by to talk about his new album Music For Existing.

Hosted by Gilla. Featuring Martyn.

item.title: A.A. Williams — The Metal Show
item.description:
The London singer/songwriter A.A. Williams joins the show to talk about her stunning new album, 'Solstice.'

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring A.A. Williams.

item.title: Karen Nyame KG — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Karen Nyame KG passes through to break down her new EP Epiphany.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Karen Nyame KG and Lakuti.

item.title: Jalen Ngonda — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Jalen Ngonda brings his soulful energy to Bandcamp Selects

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Jalen Ngonda.

item.title: Era Of Peace — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
JWords returns to the Hip Hop Show to reflect on her new album, "Sound Therapy." — a solo project born from her Saturn return, built on portable hardware, and rooted in Electronic sounds as healing.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring JWords.

item.title: Winston Hightower — The Indie Show
item.description:
Guest DJ Martin Douglas talks to Winston Hightower about new record "100 Acre Wood."

Hosted by Martin Douglas. Featuring Winston Hightower.

item.title: Seu Jorge — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Seu Jorge joins us to talk about his new album The Other Side.

Hosted by Gilla. Featuring Seu Jorge.

item.title: Northwest Terror Fest — The Metal Show
item.description:
A wide array of guests joined host Brad Sanders for interviews at this year's Northwest Terror Fest in Seattle, including members of Kylesa, Dungeon Serpent, and Serpent Rider, plus fest organizer Joseph Schafer.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Kylesa, Dungeon Serpent, and Serpent Rider.

item.title: Asna — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Ivorian DJ and producer Asna joins Emily to talk about her new EP Bring Back Rave In Town

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Asna and Kate Hutchinson.

item.title: Thee Marloes — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Indonesia's Thee Marloes mark their sophomore album, Di Hotel Malibu.

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Thee Marloes.

item.title: This For U — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
Ahwlee, the Long Beach beatmaker joins the show to chat FTRS_pt.6 – six chapters deep into one of underground hip hop's most essential series. We sat down to talk craft, inspiration, and making music entirely on his own terms.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Ahwlee.

item.title: The Lemon Twigs — The Indie Show
item.description:
It's a '60s hit parade when The Lemon Twigs stop by The Indie Show. Satisfaction guaranteed!

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring The Lemon Twigs.

item.title: Blue Lab Beats — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Blue Lab Beats join us to talk about a decade in music, their community roots and making the most 'London' album that they could.

Hosted by Gilla. Featuring Blue Lab Beats

item.title: At the Gates — The Metal Show
item.description:
Jonas Björler joins the show to discuss the powerful final album from At the Gates and the musical legacy of the band's fallen frontman, Tomas Lindberg.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring At the Gates.

item.title: Branko and Kalaf Epalanga — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Branko and Kalaf Epalanga join Emily to celebrate 20 years of their label Enchufada and of their pioneering band Buraka Som Sistema. They talk about doing it DIY, building a global community and the cultural element of club music.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Branko and Kalaf Epalanga and Mahnoor

item.title: Esa Williams — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
London-based South African producer, DJ and sound designer Esa Williams drops by

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Esa Williams.

item.title: The Father & Magician — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
On this episode, Des Moines rapper Teller Bank$ joins the show to unpack the raw & personal world of his newest release, "Hate Island." With over 30 releases and an unrelenting creative vision, he's added another gem to his dynamic catalog.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Teller Bank$.

item.title: White Fence — The Indie Show
item.description:
Tim Presley talks about the new record from White Fence plus we hear fresh tracks from Horse Lords, Las Robertas, Rosali, and more.

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring White Fence.

item.title: Bel Cobain — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Bel Cobain stops by to talk about her new EP Kizzy.

Hosted by Gilla. Featuring Bel Cobain.

item.title: Immolation — The Metal Show
item.description:
Immolation guitarist Bob Vigna discusses the New York death metal band's 35-year reign and the making of their latest album.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Immolation.

item.title: Kiioto — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Kiioto talk about the release of their second album Black Salt, touching on deeply human themes like space, heritage and time, as well as how it differs from their previous work with Lamb and Urban Cookie Collective in the 90s.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Kiioto and Daferwa

item.title: Pigeon — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Margate band Pigeon celebrate the release of their hotly-anticipated debut album, OUTTANATIONAL.

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Pigeon.

item.title: Seed Of The Holy Mecca — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
Bronx's very own, Mark Saunders joins the show to discuss his new EP, "In Due Time." Mark shares insight into his inner world, the emotional layers within his music, and what it means to trust your timing as it unfolds.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Mark Saunders.

item.title: Weird Nightmare — The Indie Show
item.description:
Alex Edkins of Weird Nightmare talks latest full-length "Hoopla" plus we hear new tracks from Spacemoth, Hedge Burners, and more.

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring Weird Nightmare.

item.title: Ego Ella May — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Ego Ella May stops by to talk about her new album Good Intentions.

Hosted by Gilla. Featuring Ego Ella May.

item.title: Commitment — The Metal Show
item.description:
Tati Salazar and Pierce Jordan from Philadelphia hardcore scorchers Commitment join the show to talk about the band's debut LP.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Commitment.

item.title: Love In The Endz — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
Love In The Endz showcase their latest release with TLS PRODUÇÕES collective from Lisbon

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring Love In The Endz presenting TLS PRODUÇÕES and La Dame

item.title: Simon Armitage (L.Y.R) — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
The UK's poet laureate Simon Armitage celebrates the new album from his band L.Y.R.

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring L.Y.R.

item.title: The Truth Speaker — The Hip Hop Show
item.description:
This week, Ras Kass joins us in conversation around his latest release Leopard Eats Face—exploring lyricism, legacy, and what it means to create with intention in today’s world.

Hosted by Stoney Creation. Featuring Ras Kass.

item.title: The Reds, Pinks & Purples — The Indie Show
item.description:
Glenn Donaldson of The Reds, Pinks & Purples chats with Mariana about his new full-length, the San Francisco music scene, and more.

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring The Reds, Pinks & Purples.

item.title: Eric Daino (The Holophonics) — The Game Show
item.description:
The Holophonics' Eric Daino breaks down the band's recent "Parking Garage Rally Circuit" OST on The Game Show's inaugural episode.

Hosted by Zoe Camp. Featuring The Holophonics.

item.title: Exodus — The Metal Show
item.description:
Exodus guitarist Gary Holt discusses the thrash band's four-decade career and their triumphant new album, 'Goliath.'

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Exodus.

item.title: DJ Marfox — Bandcamp Electronic
item.description:
DJ Marfox joins to talk about the new release DJs Di Guetto Vol II on PRÍNCIPE, the importance of community and how a chance meeting on the train changed everything.

Hosted by Emily Dust. Featuring DJ Marfox and San Farafina

item.title: Shy One — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
We're joined by Shy One to mark her sophomore album, Mali.

Hosted by Tina Edwards. Featuring Shy One.

item.title: Memorials — The Indie Show
item.description:
UK duo Memorials talk about their latest album, "All Clouds Bring Not Rain."

Hosted by Mariana Timony. Featuring Memorials.

item.title: Joshua Idehen — Bandcamp Selects
item.description:
Joshua Idehen talks about unexpected success, his storied back catalogue and his brand new LP.

Hosted by Aly Gillani. Featuring Joshua Idehen.

item.title: Final Gasp — The Metal Show
item.description:
Jake Murphy from the Boston deathrock/heavy metal band Final Gasp joins the show to discuss the band's sophomore LP.

Hosted by Brad Sanders. Featuring Final Gasp.
